rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks only into the target implementation scope when binding to implementation-level outputs or subcomponent connections.
Preserve AADL architecture by not introducing new ports, types, or package references outside the component’s visible scope.
generic_rules: Place contracts in component type scope only for visible features; use implementation scope for bindings involving subcomponents or internal state.
Ensure all AGREE variables and expressions are drawn exclusively from visible AADL features or locally declared helpers within the same annex.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` and contain only valid declarations: assume, guarantee, eq, const, or assign.
Use `int`, `real`, or `bool` as scalar types; never use AADL property names or unit tokens as AGREE identifiers.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports or internal paths in component type annexes.
Never invent declarations for unresolved identifiers—delete invalid clauses instead of extending the architecture.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations as AGREE variables
